name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَـٰلِحًا ۗ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم بَيِّنَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ هَـٰذِهِۦ نَاقَةُ ٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ ءَايَةً ۖ فَذَرُوهَا تَأْكُلْ فِىٓ أَرْضِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (٧٣)
```

```english
And to [the people of] Thamud [We sent] Salih, their brother. He said, ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. There has certainly come to you a manifest proof from your Lord. This she-camel of Allah is a sign for you. Let her alone to graze [freely] in Allah’s land, and do not cause her any harm, for then you shall be seized by a painful punishment. (73)
```

```arabic
وَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفَآءَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ عَادٍ وَبَوَّأَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ تَتَّخِذُونَ مِن سُهُولِهَا قُصُورًا وَتَنْحِتُونَ ٱلْجِبَالَ بُيُوتًا ۖ فَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ ءَالَآءَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ (٧٤)
```

```english
Remember when He made you successors after [the people of] ‘Ad, and settled you in the land: you build palaces in its plains, and hew houses out of the mountains. So remember Allah’s bounties, and do not act wickedly on the earth, causing corruption.’ (74)
```

```arabic
قَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوا۟ لِمَنْ ءَامَنَ مِنْهُمْ أَتَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ صَـٰلِحًا مُّرْسَلٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ ۚ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا بِمَآ أُرْسِلَ بِهِۦ مُؤْمِنُونَ (٧٥)
```

```english
The elite of his people who were arrogant said to those who were oppressed—to those among them who had faith—‘Do you know that Salih has been sent by his Lord?’ They said, ‘We indeed believe in what he has been sent with.’ (75)
```

```arabic
قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوٓا۟ إِنَّا بِٱلَّذِىٓ ءَامَنتُم بِهِۦ كَـٰفِرُونَ (٧٦)
```

```english
Those who were arrogant said, ‘We indeed disbelieve in what you have believed.’ (76)
```

```arabic
فَعَقَرُوا۟ ٱلنَّاقَةَ وَعَتَوْا۟ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ وَقَالُوا۟ يَـٰصَـٰلِحُ ٱئْتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَآ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (٧٧)
```

```english
So they hamstrung the She-camel and defied the command of their Lord, and they said, ‘O Salih, bring us what you threaten us with, if you are one of the apostles.’ (77)
```

```arabic
فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دَارِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ (٧٨)
```

```english
Thereupon the earthquake seized them, and they lay lifeless prostrate in their homes. (78)
```

```arabic
فَتَوَلَّىٰ عَنْهُمْ وَقَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ لَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ رِسَالَةَ رَبِّى وَنَصَحْتُ لَكُمْ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا تُحِبُّونَ ٱلنَّـٰصِحِينَ (٧٩)
```

```english
So he abandoned them [to their fate], and said, ‘O my people! Certainly I communicated to you the message of my Lord, and I was your well-wisher, but you did not like well-wishers.’ (79)
```

## (Commentary)

His saying, the Exalted: **وَإِلى ثَمُودَ أَخاهُمْ صالِحاً** "And to Thamud [We sent] Salih, their brother" until the end of the verse. Thamud was an ancient Arab nation that inhabited the land of Yemen in al-Ahqaf. Allah sent to them "their brother Salih" (أَخاهُمْ صالِحاً), who was one of them. So **قالَ يا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ ما لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرُهُ** "he said, ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him.’" [Qarai 7:73]. He called them to monotheism (التوحيد) — *the absolute oneness of Allah in His essence, attributes, and acts* — as they were polytheists (مشركين) worshipping idols in the same manner that Nuh and Hud (peace be upon them) had called their respective polytheistic peoples.

His saying: **قَدْ جاءَتْكُمْ بَيِّنَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ** "There has certainly come to you a manifest proof from your Lord" [Qarai 7:73], meaning a witness whose testimony is decisive. This is clarified by His saying, which points to the proof itself: **هذِهِ ناقَةُ اللهِ لَكُمْ آيَةً** "This she-camel of Allah is a sign for you" [Qarai 7:73]. This was the she-camel that Allah brought forth for them from the mountain as a sign (آية) — *a miraculous phenomenon serving as evidence* — of his prophethood through his supplication (peace be upon him). This divine concern is the reason for attributing the she-camel to Allah, the Glorified.

His saying: **فَذَرُوها تَأْكُلْ فِي أَرْضِ اللهِ** "so leave her to graze in Allah’s earth" [Qarai 7:73]. This is a derivation (تفريع) based on the she-camel being a sign of Allah. It is a ruling that carries a degree of severity toward them, which would be followed by the "word of punishment" (كلمة العذاب) that distinguishes between every messenger and his nation. The Exalted said: **وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَسُولٌ فَإِذا جاءَ رَسُولُهُمْ قُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ بِالْقِسْطِ وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ** "There is a messenger for every nation: so when their messenger comes, judgement is made between them with justice, and they are not wronged" [Qarai 10:47]. The verse contains an allusion (تلويح) that leaving the she-camel to her own devices to graze and roam the earth was difficult for them and caused them distress. His saying: **فِي أَرْضِ اللهِ** "in Allah’s earth" hints at this; thus, he exhorted them and warned them against preventing her from roaming freely or touching her with harm, such as hamstringing or slaughtering her, for the consequence of that would be a painful punishment seizing them.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَاذْكُرُوا إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفاءَ مِنْ بَعْدِ عادٍ** "And remember when He made you successors after ‘Ad" [Qarai 7:74] until the end of the verse. He called them to remember the favors (نعم) of Allah upon them, just as Hud had called ‘Ad to do so. He reminded them that Allah made them successors (خلفاء) who followed previous nations like ‘Ad, and He settled them in the land (بوأهم من الأرض), meaning He established them in their dwellings therein. They take

from its plains (سهولها) — and the plain (السهل) is the opposite of the mountain, so named because of the ease (سهولة) of traversing it — palaces (قصورا), which are said to be houses surrounded by walls. And they hew the mountains into houses (وينحتون الجبال بيوتا) where they seek shelter and reside.

Then He gathered all these points and summarized them in His saying: **فَاذْكُرُوا آلاءَ اللهِ** "So remember Allah’s bounties" [Qarai 7:74]. He presented this in the form of a derivation (تفريع), even though it is a summary of the preceding details, by creating an impression of distinction (إيهام المغايرة). It is as if, after commanding them to remember the favors and listing specific details of those favors as if they were unaware of them, it was said a second time: "Since Allah has bestowed upon you great bounties (آلاء) and favors like those mentioned, then remember the bounties of Allah."

As for His saying: **وَلا تَعْثَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ** "and do not act wickedly on the earth, causing corruption" [Qarai 7:74], it is coordinated (معطوف) with His saying: **فَاذْكُرُوا** "So remember," as the coordination of a necessary consequence (اللازم) with its necessitating cause (الملزوم). Acting wickedly (العثي) has been explained as corruption (الفساد), and also as agitation and excess. Al-Raghib said in *al-Mufradat*: "The terms *al-‘ayth* (العيث) and *al-‘athi* (العثي) are close in meaning, like *jadhaba* and *jabadha* (to pull), except that *al-‘ayth* is mostly used for corruption perceived by the senses, while *al-‘athi* is used for that which is perceived by judgment (حكما). It is said: *‘atha ya‘thi ‘athyan*. Based on this: **وَلا تَعْثَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ**." End quote.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **قالَ الْمَلَأُ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا مِنْ قَوْمِهِ لِلَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا لِمَنْ آمَنَ مِنْهُمْ** "The elite of his people who were arrogant said to those who were oppressed—to those among them who had faith" [Qarai 7:75] until the end of the two verses. The Glorified indicated by clarifying His saying: **لِلَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا** "to those who were oppressed" with His saying: **لِمَنْ آمَنَ مِنْهُمْ** "to those among them who had faith" that the oppressed (المستضعفين) were the believers, and that the believers only came from the oppressed; none of the arrogant elite (المستكبرين) would believe in him. The rest is clear.

His saying: **فَعَقَرُوا النَّاقَةَ وَعَتَوْا عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ** "So they hamstrung the she-camel and defied the command of their Lord" [Qarai 7:77] until the end of the verse. To hamstring (عقر) a palm tree means to cut it from its root; to hamstring a she-camel means to slaughter it, and it also means to cut its legs. Defiance (العتو) is rebellion and refusal. The verse implies the meaning of arrogance (الاستكبار) as evidenced by its being made transitive with the preposition "from" (عن). The rest is clear.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا فِي دارِهِمْ جاثِمِينَ** "So the earthquake seized them, and they lay lifeless in their homes" [Qarai 7:78] until the end of the two verses. The earthquake (الرجفة) is a violent agitation and shaking, as in the quaking of the earth or the surging of the sea. Being lifeless (الجثوم) for humans and birds is like kneeling (البروك) for a camel.

Allah has mentioned here that the cause of their destruction was that "the earthquake seized them" (أخذتهم الرجفة), while He said in another place: **وَأَخَذَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا الصَّيْحَةُ** "and the Cry seized those who were wrongdoers" [Qarai 11:67], and in another place: **فَأَخَذَتْهُمْ صاعِقَةُ الْعَذابِ الْهُونِ** "so the thunderbolt of a humiliating punishment seized them" [Qarai 41:17]. Heavenly thunderbolts (الصواعق) are never without a terrifying cry (صيحة) accompanying them, and this is usually inseparable from an earthquake (رجفة) of the ground, which is the result of the intense atmospheric vibration toward the earth.

Furthermore, hearts tremble (توجف) and limbs quake from another perspective. Thus, it appears that their punishment was a heavenly thunderbolt (صاعقة) accompanied by a terrifying cry and an earthquake in the ground or in their hearts, so they became in their land (دارهم) — meaning their city — lifeless (جاثمين), fallen upon their faces and knees.

The verse indicates that this was linked to their disbelief and their wrongdoing against one of the signs of Allah, intended as a punishment of total extirpation (عذاب الاستئصال). The verse does not focus on the physical mechanics of how it occurred. The rest is clear.
